pushback_verification: All 3 findings verified against current state. No artifacts modified between the external review and this address-reviews invocation. 0 stale suppressions. External reviewer (GPT-5 Codex) ran openspec validate --strict, bash -n on the binary + test runner, and tests/run.sh (93 passed) — independently verified the iter-1 internal review's fixes landed and looked for additional drift (found none).
anchoring_caveat_vindicated: The iter-1 internal system review's finding S3 explicitly recommended this external pass to catch what in-context anchoring suppressed. All 3 findings GPT-5 Codex surfaced were missed by the in-context internal review — strong evidence for the recommended-session/fresh-context discipline.

resolutions.external_finding_title: Latest run selection sorts by command prefix instead of embedded timestamp
resolutions.action: Added filename_ts_epoch() helper that extracts YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SSZ from .orbit-runs filenames and converts to POSIX epoch. Rewrote list_orbit_runs() to sort entries by the extracted timestamp using a tab-separated sort key (printf '%s\t%s', sort, cut -f2-) instead of full-filename sort. Now latest_orbit_run() correctly picks the timestamp-newest JSON regardless of command prefix.
resolutions.regression_test: tests/run.sh test_recommendation_spec: 'W1 regression' — fixture with review-system-2026-05-20T09-00-00Z.json + address-reviews-2026-05-20T10-00-00Z.json; verifies tier-1 reason contains the address-reviews JSON's content (NEWER), not the review-system's (OLDER, alphabetically later)

resolutions.external_finding_title: Phase freshness uses JSON file mtime instead of the run timestamp
resolutions.action: Updated infer_phase rule 2 to call filename_ts_epoch() for the JSON's semantic timestamp instead of mtime_epoch() (which reads filesystem mtime — unstable under clone/cp/touch/checkout). Comparison against tasks.md mtime is unchanged.
resolutions.regression_test: tests/run.sh test_phase_model_spec: 'W2 regression' — fixture with review-system-2024-01-01T00-00-00Z.json whose FILE mtime is touched to now; phase resolves to applying (rule 3 wins because filename ts is ancient), not reviewing (rule 2 must NOT fire on mtime alone)

resolutions.external_finding_title: Default human summary omits last-touched time
resolutions.action: Added relative_time() helper that converts ISO-8601 to '2h ago' / '3d ago' / 'just now' etc. Updated emit_human's primary line composition to include a 'touched <rel>' segment derived from primary_record.last_touched, joined into the existing tasks · attention list with the same '·' separator.
resolutions.regression_test: tests/run.sh test_output_spec: 'W3 regression' — runs opsx-status against the mid-apply fixture and asserts the human output contains the literal substring 'touched'

resolutions.external_finding_title: (side-effect discovered by W1 fix) Prose next_recommended renders as empty 'Next: ' header
resolutions.action: Not in the external review's findings list, but exposed when W1's fix brought the actually-correct latest JSON into the rendering path (the address-reviews JSON has a prose next_recommended with multiple alternatives — not parseable to /opsx:<verb> token). Refactored emit_human to: if command is parseable, render 'Next: <cmd> <args>' + quoted reason (existing behavior); else render 'Next:' header + verbatim reason on the next line (new branch).
resolutions.discovered_by: visual smoke test after applying W1 fix
resolutions.regression_test: Implicitly covered by W3 regression which uses the mid-apply fixture; explicit prose-render test deferred to a follow-up

test_count_change.delta: +3 regression tests (W1 mixed-prefix sort, W2 mtime-vs-filename-ts, W3 touched-segment)

post_resolution_state.visual_smoke_check: opsx-status output: 'You are reviewing bootstrap-orbit-status-cli. 75/79 tasks · 1 attention · touched 5m ago' — correctly sourced from the latest address-reviews JSON (was sourcing from the stale review-system JSON before W1 fix); 'touched 5m ago' segment now present (was absent before W3 fix); prose 'Next:' renders cleanly (was empty 'Next: ' before the side-effect fix)

final_assessment: All 3 external system review findings resolved + 1 side-effect bug caught and fixed. 96/96 tests passing. The external review pass demonstrated the value of the anchoring-discipline: 3 real correctness/contract bugs missed by the in-context internal review were surfaced by the fresh-context external pass. Ready to archive.
next_recommended: Either (a) commit + push the 4 fixes + 3 new regression tests, then proceed to /opsx:archive bootstrap-orbit-status-cli, or (b) run another iter-2 external review pass to verify the fixes hold (orbit#13's framework: at this iteration, fresh-vs-seeded becomes a real choice — seeded with GPT-5 Codex iter-1 would verify their findings landed; fresh with a different model would catch any net-new issues from this batch). (a) is the practical close-out — both warnings were trivial fixes with regression tests; (b) is the rigorous belt-and-suspenders close-out.
